title: federal reserve / corporate credit facilities / pmccf | Research Paper | The Primary and Secondary Corporate Credit Facilities
summary: This research paper from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York documents the design, operations, and impact of the Primary Market Corporate Credit Facility (PMCCF) and Secondary Market Corporate Credit Facility (SMCCF)—the Fed's first-ever intervention in corporate bond markets. The facilities were launched in March 2020 to address severe market dislocations during the COVID-19 pandemic, including yield spread widening of approximately 250 bps for investment-grade bonds and 550 bps for high-yield bonds, and nearly $200 billion in outflows from corporate bond funds. The SMCCF made total purchases of $14 billion before ceasing operations on December 31, 2020.
